title: OCS/GCS qconf configuration schema
description: Schema describing the JSON produced and consumed by qconf -fmt json -sconf / -Mconf: the global or a host (local) configuration. Each configuration parameter is a member of the object. The standard parameters are described below; the set is open-ended (custom and host-local parameters are allowed via additionalProperties), and a host-local configuration usually contains only a subset, so no parameter is required. List parameters are arrays of strings, 'params' parameters are arrays of name/value entries, and scalar parameters are validated as a string/number/boolean union because most are typed by value.

execd_spool_dir.description: Spool directory used by the execution daemons.

mailer.description: Absolute path of the mail delivery agent.

xterm.description: Absolute path of the xterm command for interactive jobs.

load_sensor.description: Executables used as additional load sensors.

shell_start_mode.description: Default shell start mode for jobs.

min_uid.description: Minimum user id allowed to run jobs.

min_gid.description: Minimum group id allowed to run jobs.

user_lists.description: Access lists of users allowed cluster access.

xuser_lists.description: Access lists of users denied cluster access.

load_report_time.description: Interval at which execution daemons report load (time value).

max_unheard.description: Time after which an unheard-from host is considered unknown (time value).

enforce_project.description: Whether jobs must request a project.

enforce_user.description: Whether submit users must exist as user objects ('auto', true or false).

administrator_mail.description: Mail addresses notified of administrative problems.

mail_tag.description: Tag prepended to the subject of job mails, or NONE.

set_token_cmd.description: Command used to set AFS tokens, or NONE.

pag_cmd.description: Command used to set up a PAG, or NONE.

gid_range.description: Range(s) of additional group ids used to track job processes.

port_range.description: Port range(s) used by interactive job startup.

qlogin_daemon.description: Daemon used for qlogin sessions ('builtin' or a command).

qlogin_command.description: Client command used for qlogin sessions ('builtin' or a command).

rsh_daemon.description: Daemon used for qrsh sessions ('builtin' or a command).

rsh_command.description: Client command used for qrsh sessions ('builtin' or a command).

jsv_url.description: URL of the server-side job submission verifier, or NONE.

jsv_allowed_mod.description: Job attributes a JSV is allowed to modify.

gdi_request_limits.description: GDI request rate limits, or NONE.

rlogin_daemon.description: Daemon used for qrlogin sessions ('builtin' or a command).

rlogin_command.description: Client command used for qrlogin sessions ('builtin' or a command).

reschedule_unknown.description: Time before jobs on an unknown host are rescheduled (time value).

max_aj_instances.description: Maximum number of concurrently scheduled array job tasks (0 = unlimited).

max_aj_tasks.description: Maximum number of tasks in a single array job (0 = unlimited).

max_u_jobs.description: Maximum number of jobs a single user may have (0 = unlimited).

max_jobs.description: Maximum number of jobs in the system (0 = unlimited).

max_advance_reservations.description: Maximum number of advance reservations (0 = unlimited).

auto_user_oticket.description: Override tickets given to automatically created users.

auto_user_fshare.description: Functional shares given to automatically created users.

auto_user_default_project.description: Default project of automatically created users, or NONE.

auto_user_delete_time.description: Idle time after which an automatically created user is deleted (time value; 0 disables).

delegated_file_staging.description: Whether delegated file staging is enabled.

libjvm_path.description: Path to the JVM shared library used by the JVM thread, or empty.

additional_jvm_args.description: Additional arguments passed to the JVM thread, or empty.

topology_file.description: Path to a topology description file, or NONE.
